title: SUBSTANCE USE DISORDER SERVICES - 211info
text: SPRINGFIELD EDUCATION OFFICE BEHAVIORAL HEALTH & EDUCATION SERVICES SUBSTANCE USE DISORDER SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provide individual and group therapy for individuals seeking treatment for addictions, including problem gambling. Also provide peer, educational, and prevention services for adults, youth, and families. 1621 Centennial Boulevard Springfield, OR 97477 Eligibility: Unrestricted; primarily focus is Latino and immigrant population Hours: 11am-9pm Monday-Friday (541) 686-8483 2 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Main phone (541) 686-8483 ext 2 INTAKE PROCEDURE: Call for appointment FEES: OHP accepted, sliding scale fee, low or no cost DOCUMENTS: Insurance information, if applicable. Proof of income if applying for sliding scale fees LANGUAGES: English, Spanish AREA SERVED: Lane County SITE HOURS: Monday/Wednesday/Friday 8:30am-1:30pm, Tuesday/Thursday 9am-noon Services: Alcohol Use Disorder Education/Prevention * Hispanic/Latino Community Alcohol Use Disorder Education/Prevention * Immigrants Assessment for Substance Use Disorders * Hispanic/Latino Community Assessment for Substance Use Disorders * Immigrants Comprehensive Outpatient Substance Use Disorder Treatment * Hispanic/Latino Community Comprehensive Outpatient Substance Use Disorder Treatment * Immigrants Drug Use Disorder Education/Prevention * Hispanic/Latino Community Drug Use Disorder Education/Prevention * Immigrants DUI Offender Programs * Hispanic/Latino Community DUI Offender Programs * Immigrants Gambling Addiction Support Groups Mental Health Crisis Lines * Hispanic/Latino Community Mental Health Crisis Lines * Immigrants Substance Use Disorder Counseling * Hispanic/Latino Community Substance Use Disorder Counseling * Immigrants Other Services Offered At This Location: COUNSELING SETTINGS - MENTAL HEALTH CARE EDUCATIONAL PROGRAMS - ADULT EDUCATION CLASSES EDUCATIONAL PROGRAMS - YOUTH & FAMILIES If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service.
